lines.label: packed row housing (laborer/servant/merchant/burakumin)
lines.basis: 540 families x C_PACKED 690 px^2 gross (Tango-measured rows + eaves + roji)

lines.label: samurai houses in-wall
lines.basis: 2/3 of 60 samurai families x C_SPACED 2480 px^2 gross (Tango samurai-ward; rest hold extramural estates)

lines.label: governor's mansion (yamen)
lines.basis: fixed civic program floor at Tango-measured compound footprints (research.md A)

lines.label: six provincial ministries
lines.basis: fixed civic program floor at Tango-measured compound footprints (research.md A)

lines.label: temple precincts
lines.basis: fixed civic program floor at Tango-measured compound footprints (research.md A)

lines.label: minor civic (theater, flophouses, funerary, inspection, kura)
lines.basis: fixed civic program floor at Tango-measured compound footprints (research.md A)

lines.label: shops, inns, stables
lines.basis: fixed civic program floor at Tango-measured compound footprints (research.md A)

lines.label: cargo canal + dock basin
lines.basis: one in-wall water feature - Tango pond 2,865 / Nagahara canal+dock 2,834 px^2 measured

lines.label: circulation (trunk + ring road + streets + alleys)
lines.basis: 7% of interior at drawn widths (measured 6.8-7.0%; historical envelope 10-20%, research.md B)

_regression.source: pre-streets-rule nagahara.json (2026-07-19): after the water/tower siting fix the estate's slide seat (1737,1460) put its west wall 4px from the merchant street's centerline - inside the street's cleared band. The follow-up rule: compound walls may line a street but never stand in it.
